item.text: {"case_id":"brake-triage-p04","score":2,"pass_criteria_met":true,"fail_signal_observed":false,"positive_wakeup_observed":true,"first_sentence_lock":true,"verdict_commitment_anti_mush":true,"over_forced_verdict":false,"rationale":"The response immediately triggers the brake, names the proposed sorting-table confirmer as a fourth downstream manual patch, and shifts the diagnosis upstream to recurring identification, handoff, and system-state validation failure. It does allow a tightly bounded one-batch emergency confirmer, but explicitly refuses making it the default or a standing gate and requires root-cause records plus a structural repair owner and deadline. That satisfies the anti-spiral triage rather than directly adding another manual checkpoint."}
